name: Raspberry Filled Apricot Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 package yellow cake mix with pudding included (2.5 oz)
1 cup raspberry jam or spreadable fruit
1 package vanilla instant pudding mix, sugar-free (1 oz)
1 8 oz package frozen light whipped topping (thawed)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Grease and flour two 9- or 8-inch round cake pans.
3. In a large bowl, combine the cake mix, 1-1/3 cups apricot nectar, and eggs. Mix until moistened at low speed, then beat for 2 minutes at high speed.
4. Pour the batter into the prepared pans.
5. Bake for 25 to 35 minutes (or 30 to 40 minutes for 8-inch pans) or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Allow to cool for 15 minutes, then remove from pans and cool completely.
6. To assemble the cake, slice each layer horizontally to create four layers. Place one cake layer cut side up on a serving plate and spread with 1/3 cup of raspberry jam. Repeat with the second and third layers, then top with the remaining cake layer.
7. In a small bowl, combine the pudding mix, milk, and 2 tablespoons apricot nectar. Blend well.
8. Add the thawed whipped topping and beat for an additional 2 minutes.
9. Frost the sides and top of the cake. Sprinkle the toasted coconut on top.
10. Refrigerate at least 2 hours before serving. Store in the refrigerator.
